top3.sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM Patient p
JOIN Laboratory l ON p.ID = l.ID
WHERE l.ALP > (SELECT AVG(ALP) FROM Laboratory) -- Assuming abnormal means above average
AND p.Admission = 1; -- Assuming Admission = 1 means admitted to the hospital

SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM Patient p
JOIN Laboratory l ON p.ID = l.ID
WHERE l.ALP > (SELECT AVG(ALP) FROM Laboratory) -- Assuming abnormal means above average
AND p.Admission = 1; -- Assuming Admission = 1 means admitted to the hospital

SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM Patient p
JOIN Laboratory l ON p.ID = l.ID
WHERE l.ALP > 130 -- Common upper limit of normal for ALP
AND p.Admission = 1;

SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM Patient p
JOIN Laboratory l ON p.ID = l.ID
WHERE l.ALP > (SELECT MAX(ALP) * 0.95 FROM Laboratory) -- Assuming abnormal means in the top 5%
AND p.Admission = 1;

SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM Patient p
JOIN Laboratory l ON p.ID = l.ID
WHERE l.ALP > (SELECT AVG(ALP) FROM Laboratory) -- Assuming abnormal means above average
AND p.Admission = 1; -- Assuming Admission = 1 means admitted to the hospital

SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM Patient p
INNER JOIN Laboratory l ON p.ID = l.ID
WHERE l.ALP > 130 
AND p.Admission IS NOT NULL;

SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM Patient p
JOIN Laboratory l ON p.ID = l.ID
WHERE l.ALP > 130 -- Common upper limit of normal for ALP
AND p.Admission = 1;

final.sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM Patient p
JOIN Laboratory l ON p.ID = l.ID
WHERE l.ALP > (SELECT AVG(ALP) FROM Laboratory) -- Assuming abnormal means above average
AND p.Admission = 1; -- Assuming Admission = 1 means admitted to the hospital
